title: Concurrency Budgets, Backpressure, Rate Limits, and Worker Threads · Inference Brief: Rate Limit Identity Cost
summary: The service launches unbounded promises, blocks the event loop with CPU work, uses one global rate counter, buffers queues without rejection, and cannot explain overload recovery. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts rate limit identity cost increment in admission, concurrency, queue, rate, and CPU-work budget. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply rate and quota policies to trusted identities, tenant and operation cost, distributed storage, clock behavior, headers, exemptions, and failure mode.
Bound active requests, per-dependency concurrency, queue length, queue age, memory, rejection, Retry-After, and fairness separately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Rate Limit Identity Cost · Theory Rate Limit Identity Cost
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 2149 in a privacy-sensitive records API using conditional requests. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine concurrency, queue, tenant-rate, and bounded CPU-offload decisions; retain compatibility with one older generated client; then remove one trusted proxy hop and recompute authority from the socket boundary. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Apply rate and quota policies to trusted identities, tenant and operation cost, distributed storage, clock behavior, headers, exemptions, and failure mode.
